samples.question: I'm a new student and I plan to take JLP, but I don't know which Japanese level I will be placed into. How is the level decided, and where should I check the official information?
samples.answer_text: Based on the provided evidence, the placement into the Japanese Language Program (JLP) is determined by the results of a **placement test** administered by the ICU, with the final course level subject to change during the first class sessions based on in-class assessment. To check the official information for placement, you should refer to the **ICU website** (specifically the JLP program page or the International Christian University admissions FAQ) or contact the **JLP Office** at **jlp@icu.ac.jp** (Office Hours: Monday–Friday, 9:00–11:45 and 12:45–16:00). * **Evidence:** [2]
samples.gold_answer: Your JLP level is decided by a placement test that evaluates your Japanese proficiency (first-time JLP students must take it; returning students register for the next level) and may be adjusted after additional evaluation during the first class sessions. Placement-test timing: March for Spring enrollment, August for Autumn/Winter. For official information, check the ICU JLP pages and the JLP website, or contact the JLP Office (jlp@icu.ac.jp; ERB II Rm.104, Mon–Fri 9:00–11:45 & 12:45–16:00). [1][2]

samples.question: I missed the normal course registration day because I was sick. Can I still register or change my courses, and what part of the procedure should I pay attention to?
samples.answer_text: You can still register or change your courses, but you must follow specific procedures to avoid penalty. **1. Can you register?** Yes, you may register or change your courses, but you must complete the **Late Registration procedure** if you missed the normal Registration Period. **2. What to pay attention to?** * **Late Registration Fee:** You must pay the Late Registration Fee of **3,000 yen**. * **Ticket:** You need to buy a ticket (shoshi) for 3,000 yen on the second floor of the Administration Building. * **Interview:** You must attend the interview with your advisor between **December 5 (Fri) 9:00 and December 12 (Fri) 10:00** (Online input). * **Important Dates:** The Office is closed on **April 11 (Sat) and 18 (Sat)** for the Late Registration period. * **Verification:** After payment, you must click the **"SAVE"** button to complete the procedure. The registration change is not accepted after the designated period unless you complete the regular Registration Procedures on Registration Day. **Citation:** [1] Late Registration Procedure for students who missed the Registration Period. [2] Late Registration Procedure for students who missed the Registration Period. [3] Late Registration Procedure for students who missed the Registration Period. [4] Late Registration Procedure for students who missed the Registration Period.
samples.gold_answer: Yes. If you missed the regular Registration Day you must use the Late Registration procedure (online input + advisor interview/approval) and pay the Late Registration fee (3,000 yen) — buy a ticket on the 2nd floor of the Administration Building and pay at the Educational Affairs Group before the interview; contact your advisor in advance to meet deadlines [DOC-000730::structural_fallback_v1::P0001]. If you already completed registration but need to add/cancel courses, do so during the Registration Change Period and be sure to click the “SAVE” button to complete the change; after the change period no changes are accepted [DOC-000108::structural_fallback_v1::P0007].

samples.question: If I want to withdraw from a course after seeing my workload, what rules or deadlines should I check before I make the decision?
samples.answer_text: Before withdrawing from a course in an ICU setting, you must verify the following: 1. **Registration Change Period**: You cannot withdraw after the Registration Change period has ended. The Registration Change period has already over, so no registration change is approved. [1] 2. **Late Registration Procedure**: If you missed the Registration Period, you must complete registration through the Late Registration procedure. You must pay the Late Registration Fee (3,000 yen) before proceeding to the registration interview with your advisor. [3] 3. **Course Eligibility and Requirements**: You must have Pre-Registered for the course and made a decision to take it before the term starts. Absence due to additional registration will not be a valid excuse. [4] 4. **Course Specifics**: For sport-related courses, there may be introductory and intermediate courses. You may be required to take the introductory course before registering for the intermediate course. [4] 5. **Health and Physical Education Courses**: Health and Physical Education Courses are recommended, but you must complete the regular registration procedures on Registration day. [2] 6. **Final Confirmation**: After registration and registration change, please confirm that the courses you wish to add and/or cancel is reflected correctly via the Registration Website "Registration Results". [2] **Conclusion**: Before making the decision to withdraw, check the Registration Change period, ensure you have completed the Late Registration procedure with the correct fee, verify your Pre-Registration status, and confirm the final course selection on the Registration Website.
samples.gold_answer: Check the Registration Change deadline — no registration changes are approved after the Registration Change period ends and no retroactive credit will be given; failing to drop a course (i.e., remaining absent without completing the drop) can result in an F [1]. Be sure to complete the Registration Change properly by clicking the “SAVE” button and confirming your Registration Results [2]. If you miss the regular period, verify Late Registration procedures, deadlines, advisor approval, and the late‑registration fee (e.g., ¥3,000) [3]. Also note that a Registration Withdrawal (which withdraws ALL courses) requires dean permission for illness/unavoidable reasons [1].

samples.question: I am trying to plan my major requirements and study abroad at the same time. Where should I look to confirm whether courses or credits will count toward my ICU requirements?
samples.answer_text: Based on the provided evidence, the primary method for confirming whether courses or credits will count toward ICU requirements is through the **Graduation Requirement Checklist** and the **ICU Admissions Center** process. **Evidence:** [3] FD Newsletter Vol. 29 states that graduates should consult the "Graduation Requirement Checklist on icuMAP" to confirm the credits required for each category. It also recommends consulting IBS advisors for scheduling and job-hunting support, indicating that admissions officers actively review and tailor course plans to ensure graduation compliance. [3] **Evidence:** [1] and [4] confirm that coursework completed as a Kenkyusei or in an unaccredited school may not count toward ICU's residency requirement if specific conditions are met. Specifically, if a Kenkyusei wishes to become a Regular/Transfer Student, the coursework must be submitted to the ICU Admissions Center for screening, and the coursework completed as a Kenkyusei will **not be applied toward ICU's residency requirement** [1]. Additionally, for international applicants, the required documents include a list of courses progressing or to be taken in the final semester/year to verify graduation compliance, and the original or certified true copies of such a diploma must be submitted upon admission to confirm eligibility [4]. **Conclusion:** You should look to the **ICU Admissions Center** for the official screening of coursework and the submission of the **Graduation Requirement Checklist** to confirm credits. If your coursework is from an accredited institution, it will count toward your ICU residency requirement. If it is from an unaccredited school or a Kenkyusei, it may not count depending on the specific transfer status and application details [1].
samples.gold_answer: Check the Graduation Requirement Checklist on icuMAP and discuss your course plan with APS (and consult IBS advisors) — APS reviews students’ graduation requirements and confirms which credits/courses count toward each requirement [1]. Note that study-abroad credits are reviewed after you return and, for partner institutions, up to 40 credits may be transferred and applied to ICU requirements [2].

samples.question: I am worried about senior thesis deadlines. Where can I find the official schedule, and what kind of thesis-related deadlines should I expect to check?
samples.answer_text: Based on the provided evidence, here is the information regarding senior thesis deadlines and checklists: **1. Official Schedule and Registration Check** * **Schedule:** You should check the **STH391:Senior Thesis schedule** found on icuMAP. This schedule is available by clicking on a faculty's name under "Faculty Information (office hour)" > [Subjects] (selecting "AY" and "Term"). * **Registration:** You must register for your courses in the specific term (Spring, Autumn, or Summer) corresponding to your application. You cannot register for courses in the same period as your STH391 without time conflicts. * **Confirmation:** Always confirm your senior thesis advisor on the "Advisor's Info" of icuMAP. **2. Expected Deadlines** * **Submission Deadline:** Senior thesis electronic data (PDF) must be submitted online between **January 9 (Tuesday)** and **February 1 (Thursday), 2024**. * **Late Submission:** No thesis will be accepted after **16:00** on the deadline date under any circumstances. **3. Graduation Requirements Checklist** * You are responsible for checking your own graduation requirements. * You must refer to the **"Graduation Requirements Checklist"** of icuMAP. * You should register the necessary courses based on your academic planning. **Note:** Evidence indicates that while the STH391 schedule is provided, the "Graduation Requirements Checklist" remains the definitive source for confirming all graduation requirements.

samples.question: If I want to withdraw from a course after seeing my workload, what rules or deadlines should I check before I make the decision?
samples.answer_text: To withdraw from a course after seeing your workload, you should verify the following deadlines and rules before making the decision: 1. **Registration Change Period:** You must check the deadline for registration changes to ensure the course change is approved before making a decision [1]. 2. **Late Registration Procedures:** If you miss the Registration Period, you must complete registration through the Late Registration procedure, as you cannot ask for special accommodations just because your absence took place during the first week of the term [3]. 3. **Registration Deadline:** If you cannot continue your study for an ongoing term due to an absence, you must take a leave of absence or withdraw from the university [2]. 4. **Course Eligibility:** You must have Pre-Registered for that course and have made a decision to take it before the term starts [4].

samples.question: If I want to withdraw from a course after seeing my workload, what rules or deadlines should I check before I make the decision?
samples.answer_text: You must check if the Registration Change period has passed, as no changes are approved after it ends [1]. If the period is over, you cannot officially drop the course without receiving an F grade if absent [1]. If you miss the designated registration period, you must complete the Late Registration procedure between September 5 and September 12, which requires payment [3]. Ensure you click the "SAVE" button to finalize any changes [2].
samples.reasoning_text:
Thinking Process:

1.  **Analyze the Request:**
    *   Task: Answer an ICU institutional question using *only* the provided evidence.
    *   Constraint 1: Keep the final answer concise.
    *   Constraint 2: Cite evidence using [1], [2], etc.
    *   Constraint 3: If evidence is incomplete, say what is missing.
    *   Constraint 4: Do not restate the evidence or instructions in the final answer.
    *   Question: "If I want to withdraw from a course after seeing my workload, what rules or deadlines should I check before I make the decision?"

2.  **Analyze the Evidence:**
    *   [1] ehandbook - FAQ:
        *   Registration Change period is over? NO registration change approved after Registration Change period finished.
        *   No grade/credit retroactively if not officially registered during specified period.
        *   If student remains absent without dropping procedures -> F grade.
    *   [2] ehandbook - August 28, 2024 Important Information:
        *   Not complete registration during designated period -> leave of absence or withdraw.
        *   Must click "SAVE" to complete registration change.
        *   Confirm courses added/canceled are reflected.
    *   [3] AY2025 Autumn Term Registration – Course Registration Procedures:
        *   Should attend all courses from first week.
        *   No special accommodations for absence during first week.
        *   Late Registration procedure exists (Period: Sept 5-12), requires payment.
    *   [4] ehandbook - July 20, 2023 Important Information:
        *   Exercise courses: Pre-registration required before term starts

Considering the limited time, I will answer directly now.
samples.gold_answer: Check the Registration Change deadline — no registration changes are approved after the Registration Change period ends and no retroactive credit will be given; failing to drop a course (i.e., remaining absent without completing the drop) can result in an F [1]. Be sure to complete the Registration Change properly by clicking the “SAVE” button and confirming your Registration Results [2]. If you miss the regular period, verify Late Registration procedures, deadlines, advisor approval, and the late‑registration fee (e.g., ¥3,000) [3]. Also note that a Registration Withdrawal (which withdraws ALL courses) requires dean permission for illness/unavoidable reasons [1].

samples.question: I am trying to plan my major requirements and study abroad at the same time. Where should I look to confirm whether courses or credits will count toward my ICU requirements?
samples.answer_text: To confirm whether courses will count toward ICU requirements, consult IBS advisors for scheduling and review the Graduation Requirement Checklist on icuMAP [3]. For study abroad planning, refer to previous session recordings and materials provided by APS [3]. Evidence is missing specific guidance on whether study abroad credits count toward major requirements for regular students versus Kenkyusei [1].
samples.reasoning_text:
Thinking Process:

1.  **Analyze the Request:**
    *   Task: Answer an ICU institutional question using *only* the provided evidence.
    *   Constraint 1: Grounded citations only ([1], [2], etc.).
    *   Constraint 2: Keep the final answer concise.
    *   Constraint 3: Do not restate the evidence or instructions in the final answer.
    *   Constraint 4: If evidence is incomplete, say what is missing.
    *   Question: "I am trying to plan my major requirements and study abroad at the same time. Where should I look to confirm whether courses or credits will count toward my ICU requirements?"

2.  **Analyze the Evidence:**
    *   [1] Discusses "Kenkyusei" (research students) eligibility. It states coursework does *not* count toward a degree for Kenkyusei. It mentions transferring students must follow a screening process and coursework completed as Kenkyusei will not be applied towards ICU's residency requirement. This is specific to Kenkyusei status, not general study abroad or major planning for regular students.
    *   [2] Discusses Admissions Requirements by Country/Region. Mentions submitting documents verifying completion of secondary education. For Transfer applicants, documents are exempted if they have a bachelor's degree. Mentions submitting a list of courses progressing or to be taken in the final semester/year to verify course requirements for admission.
    *   [3] FD Newsletter Vol. 29. Mentions APS (Admissions/Student Services?) presents advice for course planning before studying abroad. Directs students to watch recordings/materials from previous sessions. Recommends consulting **IBS advisors** for scheduling. Mentions APS staff review the Graduation Requirement Checklist on **icuMAP** for graduation requirement concerns

Considering the limited time, I will answer directly now.
samples.gold_answer: Check the Graduation Requirement Checklist on icuMAP and discuss your course plan with APS (and consult IBS advisors) — APS reviews students’ graduation requirements and confirms which credits/courses count toward each requirement [1]. Note that study-abroad credits are reviewed after you return and, for partner institutions, up to 40 credits may be transferred and applied to ICU requirements [2].

samples.question: I am worried about senior thesis deadlines. Where can I find the official schedule, and what kind of thesis-related deadlines should I expect to check?
samples.answer_text: To find the official schedule, check the STH391:Senior Thesis schedule of each faculty on icuMAP under Faculty Information > Subjects [3], [4]. Thesis-related deadlines include Senior Thesis Research Candidacy approval before starting research [2], STH391 registration allotment based on start term (April/September) [2], [3], and avoiding time conflicts with other courses during the thesis period [3], [4]. You must also verify graduation requirements and registration changes independently [1].
